texto: Sleep my baby, rock-a-bye,On the edge you must not lie.Wolf the Fluffy roams astray,Will he grab you, drag away?Into Furthest Darkest Woods,Hide you under Willow roots?There birdies chirp and squeak,Will they let you fall asleep?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Stanislaw Sielicki que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Sleep my baby, rock-a-bye,On the edge you must not lie.Wolf the Fluffy roams astray,Will he grab …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, fantasy, folklore, com foco em reflexao-noturna e folklore.

texto: Vila the White,Built a City up height,Not in the Heavens, not on the ground,But on the edge of a Cloud,Vila the White,Put defenses the bright:Gold defends the heights, Sun defends the gate,Moon defends the City when it's late,Vila the White,Stood with Sun at sight,Watching what comes from the bay,And saw Lightning and Thunder play,Vila the White,Wed her son on Moon at night,And gave her daughter to Gold, as bride,They have couple brothers, she's their brother's wife.
explicacao: Citação de Stanislaw Sielicki sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Vila the White,Built a City up height,Not in the Heavens, not on the ground,But on the edge of a …” articula reflexao, fairytale, fantasy em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
